judgment.summary: Bloom Energy shows exceptional revenue growth and strong analyst estimates, but faces significant valuation concerns and a very high debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter EPS was $0.44 vs estimate $0.13, a surprise of 242.4%. Next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth of 130.4% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (286.1%) and fiscal year (179.5%) are exceptionally high.
Gross margin of 30.1% and operating margin of 9.6% demonstrate solid operational efficiency.
Current ratio of 5.03 and quick ratio of 3.98 suggest excellent short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $265.52M shows positive cash generation from operations.
negatives: Forward P/E of 67.28x and PEG ratio of 1.38 suggest the stock is trading at a premium valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 32.79x is very high, indicating a rich valuation relative to revenue.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 is extremely high, indicating significant financial leverage.
Profit margin of 0.2% is exceptionally low, raising concerns about profitability.
Return on equity of 1.3% and return on assets of 3.1% are very weak, indicating poor capital efficiency.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) is experiencing a mixed news cycle as it partners with Nebius to power AI infrastructure, a move that has seen its shares jump alongside other hydrogen-related companies. However, recent reports also indicate significant insider selling, suggesting some caution from within the company. Analysts are also questioning if BE is among the best climate change stocks to buy, adding to the mixed sentiment.
